name: getJira
description:
ALWAYS USE THIS TOOL to access Jira. Always execute a fresh Jira operation - never reuse issue data from previous calls. If a previous call failed, retry with corrected parameters. Supports high-level ops (LIST/TASK/STATUS/CREATE/DELETE/UPDATE) and raw API requests. Uses configured Jira credentials and normalizes JQL automatically. LIST returns {key, summary, status} plus pagination metadata. IMPORTANT: Never invent, guess, or hallucinate a Jira project key. If the correct project key is not explicitly known from user input or trusted tool results, omit any project restriction from your request and let the tool/config handle scoping.
MULTI-WORKER: Multiple agents may operate on DIFFERENT issues in parallel safely. Concurrent UPDATE/STATUS calls on the SAME issue cause conflicts - serialize writes to the same issue.

LIST PAGINATION: Every LIST response includes total_count (total issues), total_pages, current_page (1-based), page_size, has_more, next_start_at, and a pages array [{page_number, start_at}] with the entry point for every page. Use pages to dispatch all pages as parallel workers in one batch.
policyHint: For any Jira operation - listing, creating, updating, or checking issues - use this tool immediately without asking for confirmation. Never guess a project key; if uncertain, omit it.

op.description: High-level operation. If omitted, raw request mode is used via the 'json' field.

jql.description: For LIST: optional JQL filter. Do not invent or guess a project key in JQL. If the project is not explicitly known, omit any project clause and let tool/config scoping handle it. Project scoping is auto-enforced unless meta.allowCrossProject=true.

resolutionName.description: Optional resolution to set during STATUS (e.g. 'Done', 'Won't Fix').

forceDelete.description: For DELETE: true to perform hard delete; otherwise a transition to 'Done' is attempted.

maxResults.description: For LIST: maximum results per page (default 50).

startAt.description: For LIST: pagination offset — issue index to start from (0-based). Use next_start_at from a previous response, or start_at from the pages array for parallel fetching.

fields.description: For CREATE/UPDATE: Jira REST fields payload (summary, issuetype, etc.).

json.description: Raw low-level Jira request (alternative to op-mode). Proxy enforces base URL. Never invent or guess a project key in path, url, query, or body; if uncertain, omit it and let tool/config scoping handle it.

body.description: Request body as a JSON string.
